sourceMovie.title: National Lampoon's Vacation
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto National Lampoon's Vacation (1983) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The comedy and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Cars
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If National Lampoon's Vacation clicked for you, Cars (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and adventure, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after National Lampoon's Vacation?
faq.answer: Try Cars, Vacation, Sex Drive—each shares DNA with National Lampoon's Vacation while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is National Lampoon's Vacation?
faq.answer: National Lampoon's Vacation (1983) is best described as Comedy, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is National Lampoon's Vacation part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "National Lampoon's Vacation Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
